title: Whirring Wheels
content:
Lord, when on my bed I lie,
    Sleepless, unto Thee I'll cry;
    When my brain works overmuch,
    Stay the wheels with Thy soft touch.

    Just a quiet thought of Thee,
    And of Thy sweet charity,--
    Just a little prayer, and then
    I will turn to sleep again.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Arthur Dunkerley (John Oxenham), titled "Whirring Wheels", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
